title: hypericum quadrangulum subsp. maculatum
intro: Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. maculatum, also known as spotted St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family, characterized by its spotted leaves and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential in reducing stress and anxiety. Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. maculatum can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and topical creams, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
